decks.notes: The deck focuses on assembling The Fantasticar combo as quickly as possible by utilizing Doomsday and various mana acceleration spells. It also includes protection spells like Force of Will and Thoughtseize to ensure the combo goes off without interruption. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student helps with card selection and Misdirection and Duress provide additional disruption. The overall strategy is to set up the combo and win the game with Thassa's Oracle. The deck aims to be consistent and efficient in executing the combo to secure a victory.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures, such as Atraxa, Grand Unifier and Archon of Cruelty, and then bringing them back into play using spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead. It also includes disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base is optimized to support the combo pieces and ensure smooth gameplay. Overall, the deck aims to create explosive plays and overwhelm the opponent with massive threats early in the game.

The key concept of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying efficient threats to close out the game quickly. By utilizing cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will to control the opponent's resources, along with tempo cards like Daze and Wasteland to disrupt their mana base, the deck aims to keep the opponent off balance. Meanwhile, aggressive creatures such as Nethergoyf and Murktide Regent put pressure on the opponent's life total, ultimately leading to victory through a combination of disruption and aggression.

The deck revolves around quickly dumping powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty into the graveyard, then reanimating them for a devastating early game advantage. Cards like Unmask, Thoughtseize, and Collective Brutality help control the opponent's hand and disrupt their game plan. With access to powerful reanimation spells like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures before they can establish their own board presence. The deck is focused on executing its combo efficiently and quickly to secure a win.

The deck focuses on disrupting opponents' strategies while applying pressure with efficient creatures and spells. By utilizing hand disruption, countermagic, and removal spells, the deck aims to control the pace of the game and protect its threats. Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares serve as powerful threats that can quickly close out games. The deck also includes efficient cantrips like Ponder and Brainstorm to ensure consistent card draw and selection. With a mix of disruption and aggression, the deck aims to outpace opponents and secure victory through tempo advantage.

This deck focuses on aggressively controlling the board using a combination of powerful creatures and disruptive spells. By leveraging the synergy between Aether Vial and creatures with powerful enters-the-battlefield effects, the deck can quickly establish a dominant board presence while disrupting opponent's strategies with cards like Force of Will and Swords to Plowshares. With access to a wide range of utility creatures and versatile removal options, the deck aims to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through efficient and strategic gameplay.

The deck focuses on using Show and Tell to quickly put powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn or Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play for free, taking advantage of Omniscience to cast additional spells at no cost. It includes counter spells like Force of Will and Spell Pierce to protect the combo, along with cards like Veil of Summer for additional protection. The deck also includes card draw and manipulation effects like Brainstorm and Ponder, as well as cards like Cunning Wish to search for specific answers if needed.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly assemble and cast Doomsday, utilizing cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual to generate mana, while protecting the combo with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze. Once Doomsday is cast, the player can use The Fantasticar to draw a specific sequence of cards from their library, ultimately winning the game with Thassa's Oracle. The deck aims to win the game efficiently and explosively, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Will.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing land-based strategies. By using cards like Wasteland, Thespian's Stage, and Life from the Loam, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's mana base while quickly developing its own lands. The inclusion of cards like Exploration and Mox Diamond allows the deck to ramp up its mana production to play powerful spells like Malevolent Rumble and Urza's Saga. The deck also includes utility lands like Karakas and Glacial Chasm to protect key creatures or prevent damage.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures such as Griselbrand and Atraxa, Grand Unifier, using cards like Stitcher's Supplier and Collective Brutality. Then, by using cards like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Exhume, the deck aims to bring these creatures back onto the battlefield at a low cost, bypassing their usual mana cost. This allows the deck to quickly overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats and potentially win the game in just a few turns.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize cards like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to quickly ramp up mana and cast powerful spells like The Fantasticar. The deck also includes cards like Force of Will and Daze for protection, along with Thoughtseize and Consign to Memory for disruption. Quantum Riddler and Flow State are used to draw cards and maintain card advantage. The overall goal is to assemble the necessary pieces for a combo finish using The Fantasticar while controlling the game with counterspells and disruption.

This Aggro-Maverick deck focuses on utilizing powerful creatures like Wight of the Reliquary and Barrowgoyf to apply pressure on the opponent while disrupting their mana base with Wasteland. The deck also includes key cards like Green Sun's Zenith and Once Upon a Time to tutor for key creatures and find answers as needed. The deck can quickly ramp into powerful threats like Grist, the Hunger Tide and Frenzied Baloth while also protecting its creatures with Sylvan Safekeeper. Overall, this deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with efficient creatures and disruption.

The deck focuses on using Cloudpost/Tron lands to ramp up mana quickly, allowing for powerful control cards to be played early in the game. The key strategy is to use cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, Eye of the Storms to control the board and disrupt the opponent?s game plan. By using Eldrazi Confluence and Force of Will to deal with threats, and Stock Up to draw cards, the deck aims to establish a dominant board presence and outvalue the opponent. The synergy between the ramp and control elements is essential for the deck's success.

The deck aims to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures such as Griselbrand and Archon of Cruelty using cards like Unmask, Faithless Looting, and Thoughtseize. By utilizing reanimation spells like Reanimate, Animate Dead, and Shallow Grave, the deck can bring these creatures back onto the battlefield for a powerful advantage. Additionally, using cards like Lotus Petal and Dark Ritual helps to accelerate the deck's game plan, allowing for explosive plays. The deck also includes disruption in the form of Cabal Therapy and Collective Brutality to control the opponent's hand and board state.

The main idea of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan while deploying efficient threats to pressure them. By combining powerful disruption spells like Force of Will and Thoughtseize with aggressive creatures like Murktide Regent and Kaito, Bane of Nightmares, the deck aims to control the tempo of the game and close out the match quickly. With a mix of card advantage, removal spells, and hard-to-answer threats, this deck seeks to outmaneuver opponents and secure victory through board presence and card advantage.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the combo of Show and Tell or Sneak Attack to cheat out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier onto the battlefield quickly. By ramping up mana with Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal, the deck aims to overwhelm opponents with massive threats before they have a chance to respond. Utilizing cards like Brainstorm and Force of Will to protect the combo, this deck seeks to win the game through explosive plays and powerful creature synergies.
